title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible replay and versioned local state and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
Order the Schema Migration Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Schema Migration.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Schema Migration assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Schema Migration, keep Schema Migration connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Asteroids team handles case 9764 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Schema Migration · fault isolation · Debug Schema Migration, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Schema Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Schema Migration · fault isolation · Debug Schema Migration, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A debug Asteroids team handles case 7481 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Schema Migration · fault isolation · Debug Schema Migration, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding defaults to a dictionary is a complete save migration strategy.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Schema Migration.

Another successful Schema Migration run of the same reproducible replay and versioned local state happy path. That claim fails the schema migration · fault isolation evidence boundary for Schema Migration.

For schema migration · fault isolation, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Schema Migration for reproducible replay and versioned local state.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-schema-migration-1l5cw6" that uses case 9493 to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Schema Migration.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Schema Migration into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Schema Migration, keep Schema Migration connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-schema-migration-1l5cw6
# Competency: asteroids-schema-migration.
# Case 9493: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_schema_migration_1l5cw6(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1l5cw6 = "1l5cw6-6wc5l1-9493"
    assert evidence_variant_1l5cw6
    evidence_axes_1l5cw6 = (
        "player1l5cw6",
        "seed9493",
        "input6wc5l1",
        "frame1l5cw69493",
        "state94931l5cw6",
        "playtest6wc5l19493",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1l5cw6)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.
